terminal_screen.h(3) Library Functions Manual terminal_screen.h(3)

NAME

terminal_screen.h - Reusable "fixed header + scrolling logs" terminal screen abstraction.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct terminal_screen_config_t
Configuration for terminal screen rendering.

Typedefs


typedef void(* terminal_screen_header_fn) (frame_buffer_t *buf, terminal_size_t term_size, void *user_data)
Callback to render the fixed header portion of the screen.

Functions


void terminal_screen_render (const terminal_screen_config_t *config)
Render a terminal screen with fixed header and scrolling logs. void terminal_screen_cleanup (void)
Clean up terminal screen resources. session_log_buffer_t * terminal_screen_log_init (void)
Initialize session log buffer for terminal screens. void terminal_screen_log_destroy (void)
Standard log cleanup for terminal screens. void terminal_screen_log_clear (void)
Clear buffered logs for terminal screens. session_log_buffer_t * terminal_screen_get_log_buffer (void)
Get the current session log buffer. void terminal_screen_set_output_fd (int fd)
Set output file descriptor for terminal screens (splash/status).

Detailed Description

Reusable "fixed header + scrolling logs" terminal screen abstraction.

Provides a common pattern for rendering terminal screens with:

  • Fixed header area (caller-defined via callback)
  • Scrolling log feed below header (automatically managed)
  • Terminal size caching to avoid error log spam
  • ANSI-aware line wrapping using display_width()
  • Latest log at bottom (standard terminal behavior)

Definition in file terminal_screen.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef void(* terminal_screen_header_fn) (frame_buffer_t *buf, terminal_size_t term_size, void *user_data)

Callback to render the fixed header portion of the screen.

Parameters

buf Frame buffer to write header content into (via frame_buffer_printf, etc.)
term_size Current terminal dimensions (cached, refreshed every 1 second)
user_data Caller-provided context data

The callback should:

  • Append exactly the number of lines specified in terminal_screen_config_t.fixed_header_lines
  • Use display_width() to ensure lines don't exceed term_size.cols
  • Write to buf parameter (frame_buffer_printf, frame_buffer_append)
  • NOT clear the screen (terminal_screen_render does that)
  • NOT print the final newline if it would be line N+1 (causes scroll)

Definition at line 37 of file terminal_screen.h.

Function Documentation

void terminal_screen_cleanup (void )

Clean up terminal screen resources. Destroys the internal static frame buffer allocated by terminal_screen_render(). Call at program exit or when terminal screen rendering is no longer needed.

Definition at line 599 of file terminal_screen.c.

599                                    {
600   if (g_frame_buf) {
601     frame_buffer_destroy(g_frame_buf);
602     g_frame_buf = NULL;
603   }
604 }




References frame_buffer_destroy().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






session_log_buffer_t * terminal_screen_get_log_buffer
  (void )


Get the current session log buffer. Returns the log buffer owned
    by this module, or NULL if not initialized. Used by splash/status screens to
    register with the logger.




Returns


Pointer to log buffer, or NULL


Definition at line 628 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


628                                                            {
629   return g_session_log_buffer;
630 }










void
  terminal_screen_log_clear (void )


Clear buffered logs for terminal screens. Clears all previously
    captured log entries from the buffer. Useful when transitioning between
    screens to start fresh.


Definition at line 622 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


622                                      {
623   if (g_session_log_buffer) {
624     session_log_buffer_clear(g_session_log_buffer);
625   }
626 }






References session_log_buffer_clear().


Referenced by splash_log_clear(),
    ui_mdns_log_clear(), and ui_status_log_clear().






void
  terminal_screen_log_destroy (void )


Standard log cleanup for terminal screens. Destroys the session
    log buffer after screens are done rendering.


Definition at line 615 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


615                                        {
616   if (g_session_log_buffer) {
617     session_log_buffer_destroy(g_session_log_buffer);
618     g_session_log_buffer = NULL;
619   }
620 }






References session_log_buffer_destroy().


Referenced by splash_log_destroy(),
    ui_mdns_log_destroy(), and ui_status_log_destroy().






session_log_buffer_t * terminal_screen_log_init (void
  )


Initialize session log buffer for terminal screens. Creates a new
    session log buffer owned by this module. Splash and status screens use this
    buffer to display logs.




Returns


Pointer to created buffer, or NULL on failure


Definition at line 606 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


606                                                      {
607   if (g_session_log_buffer) {
608     return g_session_log_buffer; // Already initialized
609   }
610 
611   g_session_log_buffer = session_log_buffer_create();
612   return g_session_log_buffer;
613 }






References session_log_buffer_create().


Referenced by splash_log_init(), ui_mdns_log_init(),
    and ui_status_log_init().






void
  terminal_screen_render (const terminal_screen_config_t *
  config)


Render a terminal screen with fixed header and scrolling logs.
    Renders a screen following the pattern:



  
1.

  
Clear screen and move cursor to home (both stdout and stderr)

  
2.

  
Call render_header() callback to draw fixed header

  
3.

  
Calculate log area: rows - fixed_header_lines - 1 (prevent scroll)

  
4.

  
Fetch recent logs from session_log_buffer

  
5.

  
Calculate which logs fit (working backwards, accounting for wrapping)

  
6.

  
Display logs chronologically (oldest to newest, latest at bottom)

  
7.

  
Fill remaining lines to prevent terminal scroll

  
8.

  
Flush stdout




Parameters


Config Module Screen
  configuration (header callback, line count, etc.)


Terminal size is cached internally with 1-second refresh interval
    to avoid flooding error logs if terminal size checks fail repeatedly.


Definition at line 107 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


107                                                                     {
108   // Validate config
109   if (!config || !config->render_header) {
110     return;
111   }
112 
113   // Track startup timing (first 500ms)
114   static bool first_render = true;
115   if (first_render) {
116     g_render_start_time_ns = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() * 1000;
117     first_render = false;
118   }
119 
120   uint64_t now_ns = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() * 1000;
121   uint64_t elapsed_ms = (now_ns - g_render_start_time_ns) / 1000000;
122 
123   // Ensure cursor is visible for log-only UI (splash, status screens)
124   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
125 
126   // Update terminal size (cached with 1-second refresh interval)
127   // Always check on first call (when still at default 24x80) to get correct dimensions immediately
128   uint64_t now_us = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();
129   bool should_check = (g_cached_term_size.rows == 24 && g_cached_term_size.cols == 80) ||
130                       (now_us - g_last_term_size_check_us >= TERM_SIZE_CHECK_INTERVAL_US);
131 
132   if (should_check) {
133     terminal_size_t new_size = {0};
134 
135     // PRIORITY 1: COLUMNS and ROWS environment variables (most reliable, user-controlled)
136     const char *cols_env = getenv("COLUMNS");
137     const char *rows_env = getenv("ROWS");
138     if (cols_env && rows_env) {
139       char *endptr;
140       errno = 0;
141       long cols_val = strtol(cols_env, &endptr, 10);
142       if (*endptr == '\0' && errno == 0 && cols_val > 0 && cols_val <= INT_MAX) {
143         int cols = (int)cols_val;
144         errno = 0;
145         long rows_val = strtol(rows_env, &endptr, 10);
146         if (*endptr == '\0' && errno == 0 && rows_val > 0 && rows_val <= INT_MAX) {
147           int rows = (int)rows_val;
148           new_size.cols = cols;
149           new_size.rows = rows;
150         } else {
151           log_error("terminal_screen_render: Invalid ROWS value: %s", rows_env);
152         }
153       } else {
154         log_error("terminal_screen_render: Invalid COLUMNS value: %s", cols_env);
155       }
156     }
157 
158     // PRIORITY 2: Options-provided width/height (explicit user settings)
159     if (new_size.cols == 0 || new_size.rows == 0) {
160       const options_t *opts = options_get();
161       if (opts && opts->width > 0 && opts->height > 0) {
162         new_size.cols = opts->width;
163         new_size.rows = opts->height;
164       }
165     }
166 
167     // PRIORITY 3: Terminal auto-detection
168     if (new_size.cols == 0 || new_size.rows == 0) {
169       if (terminal_get_size(&new_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
170         // If terminal detection fails, use defaults
171         new_size.cols = 80;
172         new_size.rows = 24;
173       }
174     }
175 
176     if (new_size.cols > 0 && new_size.rows > 0) {
177       g_cached_term_size = new_size;
178     }
179     g_last_term_size_check_us = now_us;
180   }
181 
182   bool grep_entering = log_search_is_entering();
183 
184   // When transitioning out of grep mode, clear the log cache so all logs are redrawn
185   // (grep mode may have shown filtered logs, need to refresh for normal view)
186   static bool last_grep_state = false;
187   if (last_grep_state && !grep_entering) {
188     terminal_screen_clear_cache();
189   }
190 
191   // Also clear cache when pattern changes while still in grep mode (e.g., user backspaces to empty)
192   if (grep_entering && log_search_needs_rerender()) {
193     terminal_screen_clear_cache();
194   }
195 
196   last_grep_state = grep_entering;
197 
198   // Allocate or reuse frame buffer (module-level static to avoid malloc per frame)
199   if (!g_frame_buf) {
200     g_frame_buf = frame_buffer_create(g_cached_term_size.rows, g_cached_term_size.cols);
201     if (!g_frame_buf) {
202       return; // Allocation failed
203     }
204   }
205 
206   frame_buffer_reset(g_frame_buf);
207 
208   if (!grep_entering) {
209     // Normal mode: clear and redraw (all output goes into frame buffer)
210     frame_buffer_clear_screen(g_frame_buf);
211   } else {
212     // Grep mode: overwrite in place, never clear whole screen
213     frame_buffer_cursor_home(g_frame_buf);
214   }
215 
216   // Render fixed header via callback (writes into frame buffer)
217   config->render_header(g_frame_buf, g_cached_term_size, config->user_data);
218 
219   // If logs are disabled, flush and return
220   if (!config->show_logs) {
221     frame_buffer_flush(g_frame_buf);
222     return;
223   }
224 
225   // Measure actual header height by pre-rendering and counting display lines
226   int actual_header_height = config->fixed_header_lines;
227 
228   if (config->render_header) {
229     // Pre-render header to a temporary buffer to measure its display height
230     frame_buffer_t *temp_buf = frame_buffer_create(config->fixed_header_lines + 10, g_cached_term_size.cols);
231     if (temp_buf) {
232       config->render_header(temp_buf, g_cached_term_size, config->user_data);
233 
234       // Measure display height of the rendered header
235       // The buffer contains ANSI codes + text. We count newlines and account for wrapped lines.
236       int measured_height = 0;
237       const char *content = frame_buffer_get_content(temp_buf);
238       size_t buf_len = frame_buffer_get_length(temp_buf);
239       const char *buf_end = content + buf_len;
240       const char *line_start = content;
241 
242       while (content < buf_end) {
243         if (*content == '\n') {
244           // Found end of line - measure this line's display height
245           size_t line_len = content - line_start;
246           char line_buf[1024];
247           if (line_len < sizeof(line_buf)) {
248             strncpy(line_buf, line_start, line_len);
249             line_buf[line_len] = '\0';
250 
251             int line_height = display_height(line_buf, g_cached_term_size.cols);
252             if (line_height <= 0)
253               line_height = 1;
254             measured_height += line_height;
255           }
256           line_start = content + 1;
257         }
258         content++;
259       }
260 
261       if (measured_height > 0) {
262         actual_header_height = measured_height;
263       }
264       frame_buffer_destroy(temp_buf);
265     }
266   }
267 
268   // Calculate log area: total rows - actual header height - 1 (prevent scroll)
269   // When grep search is open, subtract additional row to prevent header bump
270   int log_area_rows = g_cached_term_size.rows - actual_header_height - (grep_entering ? 2 : 1);
271 
272   if (log_area_rows <= 0) {
273     // Terminal too small for logs, flush header and return
274     frame_buffer_flush(g_frame_buf);
275     if (grep_entering) {
276       log_search_render_input_line(g_cached_term_size.cols);
277     }
278     return;
279   }
280 
281   // When grep input is active, reserve 1 row for the grep input line at bottom.
282   // Subtract 1 from log_area_rows to prevent logs from overlapping the grep input.
283   int renderable_log_rows = grep_entering ? (log_area_rows - 1) : log_area_rows;
284 
285   // Fetch and filter logs
286   session_log_entry_t *log_entries = NULL;
287   size_t log_count = 0;
288 
289   if (log_search_is_active()) {
290     asciichat_error_t result = log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(&log_entries, &log_count);
291     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || !log_entries) {
292       log_entries = SAFE_MALLOC(SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE * sizeof(session_log_entry_t), session_log_entry_t *);
293       if (log_entries) {
294         log_count = session_log_buffer_get_recent(g_session_log_buffer, log_entries, SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE);
295       }
296     }
297   } else {
298     // Get logs from in-memory buffer
299     session_log_entry_t *buffer_entries =
300         SAFE_MALLOC(SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE * sizeof(session_log_entry_t), session_log_entry_t *);
301     if (buffer_entries) {
302       size_t buffer_count = session_log_buffer_get_recent(g_session_log_buffer, buffer_entries, SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE);
303       log_entries = buffer_entries;
304       log_count = buffer_count;
305     }
306   }
307 
308   // Display logs - most recent at bottom, oldest scrolled off top.
309   // Calculate a stable window: show logs that fit in available space, newest last.
310   // This ensures smooth scrolling as new logs arrive (no jumping viewport).
311   // Use renderable_log_rows when in grep mode to account for grep input line.
312   // CRITICAL: account for multi-line logs via display_height() in pre-selection.
313   // Without this, logs overflow and push the header off screen.
314   int first_log_to_display = log_count; // Start past the end (no logs selected)
315   int total_lines_used = 0;
316   int available_rows = grep_entering ? renderable_log_rows : log_area_rows;
317 
318   // Work backwards from newest to oldest log, selecting which fit
319   for (int i = (int)log_count - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
320     int lines_for_msg = display_height(log_entries[i].message, g_cached_term_size.cols);
321     if (lines_for_msg <= 0) {
322       lines_for_msg = 1;
323     }
324     // Check if adding this log would exceed available space
325     if (total_lines_used + lines_for_msg <= available_rows) {
326       total_lines_used += lines_for_msg;
327       first_log_to_display = i; // This is the first (oldest) log we'll display
328     }
329     // Continue checking older logs - they might be shorter and still fit
330   }
331 
332   // If no logs fit, show nothing (just padding)
333   if (first_log_to_display == (int)log_count) {
334     first_log_to_display = log_count; // Will result in 0 logs to display
335   }
336 
337   int logs_to_display = log_count - first_log_to_display;
338 
339   // Log detailed info about what logs are being displayed
340   int logs_displayed_count = logs_to_display;
341   if (elapsed_ms < 500) {
342     log_dev_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "[STARTUP_FRAME] t=%llu log_count=%zu first=%d display=%d area=%d",
343                   (unsigned long long)elapsed_ms, log_count, first_log_to_display, logs_displayed_count, log_area_rows);
344   } else {
345     log_debug_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT,
346                     "[HEIGHT_CALC] log_count=%zu first_log_to_display=%d logs_displayed=%d "
347                     "renderable_log_rows=%d",
348                     log_count, first_log_to_display, logs_displayed_count, renderable_log_rows);
349   }
350 
351   if (!grep_entering) {
352     // Normal mode: accumulate everything (header + logs + blank lines) into frame buffer,
353     // then flush atomically. This prevents real-time logs from appearing after the frame,
354     // which would cause the display to scroll and push the header up.
355 
356     // Output logs to frame buffer, counting actual display lines (accounting for wrapping)
357     int terminal_lines_used = 0;
358     for (int i = first_log_to_display; i < (int)log_count && terminal_lines_used < log_area_rows; i++) {
359       const char *msg = log_entries[i].message;
360 
361       // Calculate how many display lines this message will consume when wrapped
362       // Uses display_height() which handles ANSI codes and UTF-8 properly
363       int lines_for_this_msg = display_height(msg, g_cached_term_size.cols);
364       if (lines_for_this_msg <= 0) {
365         lines_for_this_msg = 1;
366       }
367 
368       // Only output if it fits in remaining space
369       if (terminal_lines_used + lines_for_this_msg <= log_area_rows) {
370         frame_buffer_printf(g_frame_buf, "%s\n", msg);
371         terminal_lines_used += lines_for_this_msg;
372       }
373     }
374 
375     // Pad remaining log area with blank lines to maintain fixed frame height.
376     // This ensures every frame has exactly the same height on screen,
377     // preventing flicker caused by dynamic frame height changes.
378     while (terminal_lines_used < log_area_rows) {
379       frame_buffer_printf(g_frame_buf, "\n");
380       terminal_lines_used++;
381     }
382 
383     // Flush entire frame (header + logs + blank lines) in one atomic write
384     frame_buffer_flush(g_frame_buf);
385   } else {
386     // Grep mode: diff-based rendering. Only rewrite lines that changed.
387     // Logs fill renderable_log_rows; the last row is the `/` input line.
388 
389     // Flush frame buffer (containing header) before rendering grep logs
390     frame_buffer_flush(g_frame_buf);
391 
392     // Suppress logging during grep rendering to prevent log messages from
393     // interleaving with escape sequences and appearing as literal text in logs
394     log_level_t saved_level = log_get_level();
395     log_set_level(LOG_FATAL); // Suppress all logging except fatal errors during rendering
396 
397     int log_idx = 0;
398     int lines_used = 0;
399     int fd = frame_buffer_get_screen_output_fd();
400     FILE *output_file = (fd == STDERR_FILENO) ? stderr : stdout;
401 
402     for (int i = first_log_to_display; i < (int)log_count; i++) {
403       const char *original_msg = log_entries[i].message;
404       const char *msg = original_msg;
405 
406       // Strip ANSI codes first to match against plain text
407       char plain_text[SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX] = {0};
408       strip_ansi_codes(original_msg, plain_text, sizeof(plain_text));
409 
410       // Apply highlighting if match found in plain text
411       size_t match_start = 0, match_len = 0;
412       if (log_search_get_match_info(plain_text, &match_start, &match_len) && match_len > 0) {
413         // Validate match is within bounds
414         size_t plain_len = strlen(plain_text);
415         if (match_start < plain_len && (match_start + match_len) <= plain_len) {
416           const char *highlighted = grep_highlight_colored(original_msg, plain_text, match_start, match_len);
417           if (highlighted && highlighted[0] != '\0') {
418             msg = highlighted;
419           }
420         }
421       }
422 
423       int msg_display_width = display_width(msg);
424       if (msg_display_width < 0) {
425         msg_display_width = (int)strlen(msg);
426       }
427       int lines_for_this = 1;
428       if (g_cached_term_size.cols > 0 && msg_display_width > g_cached_term_size.cols) {
429         lines_for_this = (msg_display_width + g_cached_term_size.cols - 1) / g_cached_term_size.cols;
430       }
431 
432       // When logs are filtered (grep active), they're in a different order than before.
433       // Only use cached pointers when NOT filtering to avoid cache misses on every frame.
434       // This prevents the "spam" effect where all logs redraw constantly during grep searches.
435       bool same_as_before = false;
436       if (!log_search_is_active()) {
437         same_as_before = (log_idx < g_prev_log_count && g_prev_log_ptrs[log_idx] == original_msg);
438       }
439 
440       if (same_as_before) {
441         // Content unchanged - skip past it without rewriting.
442         if (lines_for_this == 1) {
443           fprintf(output_file, "\n");
444         } else {
445           fprintf(output_file, "\x1b[%dB", lines_for_this);
446         }
447       } else {
448         // Content changed - overwrite and clear tail.
449         fprintf(output_file, "%s\n", msg);
450       }
451 
452       if (log_idx < MAX_CACHED_LINES) {
453         g_prev_log_ptrs[log_idx] = original_msg;
454       }
455       log_idx++;
456       lines_used += lines_for_this;
457     }
458 
459     // Fill blank lines up to renderable_log_rows. If there's one line of space left,
460     // render one more partial log (truncated to fit terminal width) instead of blank line.
461     int remaining = renderable_log_rows - lines_used;
462 
463     if (remaining >= 1 && first_log_to_display > 0) {
464       // Render one more log line above the displayed ones, truncated to fit terminal width
465       int prev_idx = first_log_to_display - 1;
466       const char *prev_msg = log_entries[prev_idx].message;
467 
468       int prev_width = display_width(prev_msg);
469       if (prev_width < 0) {
470         prev_width = (int)strlen(prev_msg);
471       }
472 
473       if (prev_width > g_cached_term_size.cols) {
474         // Truncate to fit: progressively test shorter substrings until one fits
475         int target_width = g_cached_term_size.cols - 3; // Reserve space for ellipsis
476         if (target_width <= 0) {
477           fprintf(output_file, "...\x1b[K\n");
478         } else {
479           size_t src_len = strlen(prev_msg);
480           bool found = false;
481 
482           for (size_t truncate_at = src_len; truncate_at > 0; truncate_at--) {
483             char test_buf[SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX];
484             SAFE_STRNCPY(test_buf, prev_msg, truncate_at);
485             test_buf[truncate_at] = '\0';
486 
487             int test_width = display_width(test_buf);
488             if (test_width < 0) {
489               test_width = (int)strlen(test_buf);
490             }
491 
492             if (test_width <= target_width) {
493               // Found a length that fits
494               fprintf(output_file, "%s...\x1b[K\n", test_buf);
495               found = true;
496               break;
497             }
498           }
499 
500           if (!found) {
501             fprintf(output_file, "...\x1b[K\n");
502           }
503         }
504       } else {
505         // Fits without truncation
506         fprintf(output_file, "%s\x1b[K\n", prev_msg);
507       }
508 
509       remaining--;
510     }
511 
512     // Fill remaining blank lines with color reset to prevent color bleed
513     for (int i = 0; i < remaining; i++) {
514       fprintf(output_file, "\n");
515     }
516 
517     g_prev_log_count = log_idx;
518     g_prev_total_lines = lines_used;
519 
520     // Flush buffered output before rendering grep UI to ensure correct order
521     fflush(output_file);
522 
523     // Atomic grep UI rendering: combine cursor positioning and input line into
524     // a single write to prevent log output from interrupting the escape sequences.
525     // This prevents the race condition where logs appear between the cursor
526     // positioning command and the grep input line rendering.
527     char grep_ui_buffer[512];
528 
529     // Validate terminal size before using it in formatting
530     if (g_cached_term_size.rows <= 0 || g_cached_term_size.rows > 9999) {
531       // Invalid terminal size - skip grep rendering to avoid malformed output
532       return;
533     }
534 
535     int pos = snprintf(grep_ui_buffer, sizeof(grep_ui_buffer), "\x1b[%d;1H", g_cached_term_size.rows);
536 
537     // Validate snprintf succeeded and produced expected output
538     if (pos > 0 && pos < (int)sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - 256) {
539       // Lock while reading grep state to ensure atomic render
540       // Get the search pattern under mutex protection
541       mutex_t *grep_mutex = log_search_get_mutex();
542       if (grep_mutex) {
543         mutex_lock(grep_mutex);
544         int pattern_len = log_search_get_input_len();
545         const char *pattern = log_search_get_input_buffer();
546         int cursor_pos = log_search_get_cursor_position();
547 
548         // Validate pattern_len and cursor_pos are within reasonable bounds
549         if (pattern_len < 0 || pattern_len > 256) {
550           pattern_len = 0;
551           pattern = NULL;
552         }
553         if (cursor_pos < 0 || cursor_pos > pattern_len) {
554           cursor_pos = pattern_len;
555         }
556 
557         if (pattern_len > 0 && pattern) {
558           int remaining =
559               snprintf(grep_ui_buffer + pos, sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - (size_t)pos, "/%.*s", pattern_len, pattern);
560           if (remaining > 0 && remaining < (int)sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - (int)pos) {
561             pos += remaining;
562           }
563         } else {
564           // No pattern yet - just output the slash
565           if (pos + 1 < (int)sizeof(grep_ui_buffer)) {
566             grep_ui_buffer[pos++] = '/';
567           }
568         }
569 
570         // Position cursor at the current edit position within the pattern
571         // After writing "/{pattern}", cursor is at column = 1 + pattern_len + 1
572         // We want it at column = 1 + cursor_pos + 1 (for "/" at column 1, then cursor_pos chars)
573         // So move left by: pattern_len - cursor_pos columns
574         int cursor_offset = pattern_len - cursor_pos;
575         if (cursor_offset > 0) {
576           int remaining =
577               snprintf(grep_ui_buffer + pos, sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - (size_t)pos, "\x1b[%dD", cursor_offset);
578           if (remaining > 0 && remaining < (int)(sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - (size_t)pos)) {
579             pos += remaining;
580           }
581         }
582 
583         mutex_unlock(grep_mutex);
584       }
585     }
586 
587     // Write entire grep UI (cursor positioning + input) in single operation
588     if (pos > 0 && pos <= (int)sizeof(grep_ui_buffer)) {
589       platform_write_all(fd, grep_ui_buffer, (size_t)pos);
590     }
591 
592     // Restore logging level after grep rendering completes
593     log_set_level(saved_level);
594   }
595 
596   SAFE_FREE(log_entries);
597 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, terminal_size_t::cols,
    display_height(), display_width(), errno,
    terminal_screen_config_t::fixed_header_lines,
    frame_buffer_clear_screen(), frame_buffer_create(),
    frame_buffer_cursor_home(), frame_buffer_destroy(),
    frame_buffer_flush(), frame_buffer_get_content(),
    frame_buffer_get_length(),
    frame_buffer_get_screen_output_fd(), frame_buffer_printf(),
    frame_buffer_reset(), grep_highlight_colored(),
    options_state::height, log_debug_every, log_dev_every,
    log_error, LOG_FATAL, log_get_level(),
    log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(),
    log_search_get_cursor_position(),
    log_search_get_input_buffer(), log_search_get_input_len(),
    log_search_get_match_info(), log_search_get_mutex(),
    log_search_is_active(), log_search_is_entering(),
    log_search_needs_rerender(), log_search_render_input_line(),
    log_set_level(), MAX_CACHED_LINES,
    session_log_entry_t::message, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, options_get(),
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_write_all(),
    terminal_screen_config_t::render_header,
    terminal_size_t::rows, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, session_log_buffer_get_recent(),
    SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE, SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX,
    terminal_screen_config_t::show_logs,
    TERM_SIZE_CHECK_INTERVAL_US, terminal_cursor_show(),
    terminal_get_size(), terminal_screen_config_t::user_data, and
    options_state::width.


Referenced by ui_status_display(), and
    ui_status_display_interactive().






void
  terminal_screen_set_output_fd (int fd)


Set output file descriptor for terminal screens
  (splash/status).


Parameters


fd File descriptor (STDOUT_FILENO for TTY,
  STDERR_FILENO for piped)


Configures where splash and status screens write their output.
    Should be called before terminal_screen_render() to control output
    destination.


Definition at line 632 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


632                                            {
633   frame_buffer_set_screen_output_fd(fd);
634 }
